findings.summary: REQ-T1-1 header says 'classify exactly the following 5 scenarios as RETIRE' but enumerates only 4 paths, then item 5 pirouettes into 'freeze as 4'. AC-T1-1-1 tests 4 paths. A Phase 5 agent legitimately reading the REQ header will expect 5 entries and may invent a phantom 5th or import `pipeline-history-pii-scope` from Phase 2's RETIRE=5 summary. Reconciliation is ONE SENTENCE of 'Correction note'; it is not a clean rewrite of the EARS clause.
findings.proposed_fix: Rewrite REQ-T1-1 as 'WHEN v6.10.0 is prepared, THE SYSTEM SHALL classify exactly the following 4 scenarios as RETIRE:' and delete the §5 paragraph entirely. The reconciliation note belongs in Revision Notes, not embedded in the REQ body.
findings.phase_7_failure_mode: Phase 5 fixer sees 'exactly 5' and adds a 5th scenario — most likely `v6.9.0-pipeline-history-pii-scope.sh` (which Phase 2 SUMMARY labeled RETIRE while Phase 2 TABLE labeled REWRITE). AC-T1-1-1 then fails because wc -l returns 4.

findings.summary: Spec claims baseline 'KEEP=13, REWRITE=14, EXTEND=8, RETIRE=4 (total 39 + 2 reference = 41)'. Phase 2 TABLE enumeration (ground truth, authoritative per judge) actually contains: KEEP=14, REWRITE=16, EXTEND=8, RETIRE=3. Phase 2 summary says KEEP=13/REWRITE=14/RETIRE=5 — even Phase 2 contradicts itself. Spec picked an arbitrary reconciliation without documenting WHY `pipeline-history-pii-scope` and `pipeline-paused-webhook` (Phase 2 TABLE: REWRITE) were DROPPED from REQ-T1-2's 14-list. REQ-T1-2 note casually asserts 'judge classified as REWRITE; this REQ moves to EXTEND' — but REQ-T1-3's EXTEND list does NOT contain them either. They have vanished into neither-REWRITE-nor-EXTEND limbo.
findings.proposed_fix: Enumerate all 41 v6.9.0-prefixed scenarios in an appendix with explicit classification + cite Phase 2 line for each. Explicitly call out `pipeline-history-pii-scope` and `pipeline-paused-webhook` — are they KEEP? EXTEND? dropped? The spec cannot silently orphan 2 scenarios.
findings.phase_7_failure_mode: Phase 5 agent scans `tests/scenarios/v6.9.0-*.sh`, finds `pipeline-history-pii-scope.sh` and `pipeline-paused-webhook.sh` with no classification, either modifies them (violating some unwritten contract) or leaves them (causing AC-T1-16 diff check to pass trivially while these doc-grep scenarios linger unpatched — the exact v6.9.0 miss pattern this release is supposed to fix).

findings.summary: REQ-T1-5 prohibits awk+source in 'new or rewritten v6.10.0 test scenario'. AC-T1-5-1 enumerates 'each of the 14 REWRITE targets and each NEW scenario'. Design §4.2 lists a HARDCODED bash array that Phase 5 must keep in sync with REQ lists — an invitation to manual-sync drift. CRITICAL: the enumeration scope is INCONSISTENT across the three artifacts: REQ-T1-7 says 'RETIRE-tagged + EXTEND-tagged + REWRITE-tagged + net-new'; AC-T1-5-1 says '14 REWRITEs + new'; design §4.2 says '4 RETIRE + 14 REWRITE + 8 EXTEND + net-new'. Three different enumeration surfaces for the same gate.
findings.proposed_fix: Freeze ONE enumeration surface in a single normative list (e.g., a new section 'v6.10.0-touched scenario ledger' at end of requirements.md). REQ-T1-7, AC-T1-5-1, and the design §4.2 array all reference THAT ledger by name. The touched-scenario list MUST be generated by script (e.g., `git diff --name-only v6.9.2..HEAD -- tests/scenarios/` piped through a classification filter) rather than hand-authored.
findings.phase_7_failure_mode: Phase 5 authors the anti-pattern gate with ONE of the three scope definitions; Phase 8 Commander reads a DIFFERENT one; AC passes vacuously. The awk+source code-lift in `v6.9.0-pause-timeout-validation.sh` (flagged in traceability §A row 13 — 'MUST be refactored') slips through because the gate's array omitted the EXTEND variant.

findings.summary: AC-T2-8-1 says research file 'exists AND declares HIGH confidence for JSON schema, invocation contract, exit-code semantics, trigger scope'. This is UNTESTABLE — 'declares HIGH confidence' is not a specific predicate. Is HIGH a literal string somewhere? The word 'HIGH' in ALL-CAPS? A YAML frontmatter field? A scoring rubric with a numeric threshold? Phase 5 writer can scribble 'Confidence: HIGH' at the top of an empty file and the AC passes. Worse, the research ARTIFACT IS NOT YET PRODUCED — the `.forge/phase-4-spec/research/` directory does not exist at review time, so this AC is evaluating a future artifact whose schema is unspecified.
findings.proposed_fix: Define the research artifact schema: (a) required YAML frontmatter keys `confidence: HIGH|MEDIUM|LOW`, `evidence_citations: [url, url, url]` (≥ 2 primary sources per gap); (b) required sections '## JSON schema', '## Invocation contract', '## Exit-code semantics', '## Trigger scope' each with a citation block; (c) HIGH = all 4 sections have ≥2 primary-source citations AND a Claude Code team doc URL OR a working code sample validated by hand. AC-T2-8-1 then runs a structural grep on the artifact.
findings.phase_7_failure_mode: Phase 5 research agent produces a 1-page summary ending 'Confidence: HIGH' with no external citations; AC passes; Layer 2 Script ships against a hallucinated API; hook either doesn't fire or crashes Claude Code in the field. The entire abort-lane (REQ-T2-FALLBACK) is rendered inert because the gate cannot detect inadequate research.

findings.summary: REQ-T2-3 freezes '42 dispatch sites distributed across 5 files (13+13+12+≥10+2)'. The 'scaffold ≥10, research found 15-16; range accepted' clause is a phantom number that will NOT be audited. Phase 2 research did NOT enumerate line numbers per site in spec artifacts — the line-number list is referenced as 'see Phase 2 §Dispatch-Prose Enumeration' without quoting the actual 42 line numbers INTO the REQ. Further: 'Additional sites discovered during Phase 5 review SHALL be included (the 42 count is a lower bound; drift-tolerant)' is a freeze violation — a drift-tolerant count is not a freeze at all. AC-T2-2-1 says '≥ 42', meaning Phase 5 can rewrite 50 sites and nobody verifies the right 42 are the canonical ones.
findings.proposed_fix: Enumerate all 42 sites by (file, line-number) tuples in an appendix of requirements.md. Copy the exact line numbers from Phase 2 research into the spec; don't delegate. Change AC-T2-2-1 to assert EACH enumerated site matches — not aggregate count.
findings.phase_7_failure_mode: Phase 5 sed pass misses one site in scaffold/SKILL.md, rewrites a lookalike-but-not-dispatch-prose line in core/fixer-reviewer-loop.md, aggregate count hits 42, AC passes, but the canonical-site coverage is 41/42. The unprotected site is the regression vector the release is supposed to close.

findings.summary: REQ-T1-8 says 'Phase 8 Commander SHALL verify that [gate] exists and exits zero'. AC-T1-8-1 requires 'Commander's report.md includes a line citing the gate's exit code, quoting exit 0'. This is sloppy. Commander reads WHAT specifically? Runs the gate script directly? Reads harness-output.log? The spec does not say 'Commander SHALL execute `bash tests/scenarios/v6.10.0-no-awk-source-in-rewrites.sh; echo $?` and capture stdout+exit code into report.md § 〈named heading〉'. Current phrasing lets a Commander agent generate a plausible-looking report quoting 'exit 0' from a hallucinated run.
findings.proposed_fix: Spec must say: 'Commander SHALL (a) execute the gate script via bash -c, (b) capture exit code to a dedicated field commander_gate_exit_code in report.md frontmatter, (c) grep the release-commit harness log for PASS/FAIL line matching the gate filename, (d) report BOTH sources with cross-check. A mismatch between direct execution and harness log is a release-block.'
findings.phase_7_failure_mode: Phase 8 Commander writes 'Gate exit: 0 (verified)' — no actual verification occurred. Release ships with a silently-failing gate.

findings.summary: REQ-T1-9 says post-release harness = 186 but admits 'correction note: may be 187'. Traceability.md §184 independently calculates '204 scenarios' based on '19 net-new'. Three different harness totals (186, 187, 204) in the same spec. The traceability 204 figure comes from 19 net-new test scenarios the spec produced AFTER the brainstorm set effort-budget expectations — this alone doubles the release's test-authoring scope without revising NFR-10's 42-48h budget.
findings.proposed_fix: Pick ONE harness-total target. Either (a) freeze at 186 and remove the 15 verification scenarios traceability invented, OR (b) freeze at 204 and explicitly adjust Track 1 effort from 30h to ~45h (accounting for 15 new scenarios × 1h avg). Cannot ship 19 net-new scenarios on a 30-hour Track 1 budget.
findings.phase_7_failure_mode: Phase 7 runs out of effort budget mid-track; fixer blocks issue; Phase 8 Commander reports incomplete coverage. OR the 15 verification scenarios are quietly dropped and their REQs become orphan ACs.

findings.summary: NFR-8 claims 'disclose in CHANGELOG and docs/reference/hooks.md' for T3-ADV-1/2/3 and T2-ADV-3. REQ-T3-12 + AC-T3-12-1 put T3-ADVs in `core/agent-states.md § 'Tracker content normalization — deferred to v6.11.0'`. T2-ADV-3 has NO SPECIFIC FILE + HEADING pointer in the spec. AC-T2-9-2 conditionally requires the phrase 'Autopilot dispatch audit parity' in `docs/guides/dispatch-enforcement.md` IFF research resolves 'suppressed'. If research resolves 'fires', T2-ADV-3 is silently un-disclosed. Honest disclosure should not depend on a research outcome.
findings.proposed_fix: Unconditional disclosure: 'docs/guides/dispatch-enforcement.md § Known limitations' SHALL enumerate T2-ADV-3 regardless of research outcome. The research outcome modulates the mitigation text, not the disclosure existence.
findings.phase_7_failure_mode: Research returns 'hooks fire under --dangerously-skip-permissions'; Phase 5 agent reads AC-T2-9-2 as vacuous; T2-ADV-3 is never disclosed anywhere; v6.11.0 maintainer discovers the gap a year later.

findings.summary: Traceability.md §A row 13 admits `v6.9.0-pause-timeout-validation.sh` REWRITE 'uses awk+source code-lift in Phase 2 proposal — MUST be refactored to avoid REQ-T1-5 violation'. This is a known conflict between the brainstorm's REWRITE-plan and the user-override's anti-pattern constraint. The spec's resolution ('inline parse_pause_timeout logic or re-implement as pure bash') is hand-waved — no requirement, no AC, no effort estimate. parse_pause_timeout is a non-trivial function with edge cases (min 1h, max 365d, unit parsing, invalid-value warning) that will now need a PURE-BASH re-implementation in the test scenario. That is ~3 hours of work not budgeted anywhere.
findings.proposed_fix: Add REQ-T1-19 'parse_pause_timeout re-implementation strategy' specifying whether the REWRITE tests against the live production function (requires sourcing skills/autopilot/SKILL.md blob — also prohibited) OR duplicates logic inline (code-duplication risk — a silent regression if production changes). Pick one. Budget the hours.
findings.phase_7_failure_mode: Phase 5 fixer either (a) violates REQ-T1-5 silently, (b) inlines a divergent copy of parse_pause_timeout, or (c) gives up and drops `v6.9.0-pause-timeout-validation.sh` from the REWRITE list, breaking REQ-T1-2's 14-count.

findings.summary: Pre-track ordering dependencies are listed but not enforced by any AC. AC-T2-1-2 says the grep update 'PASSES both BEFORE and AFTER Layer 1' — fine — but nothing verifies the grep update commit PRECEDES the Layer 1 sed commit in git history. REQ-T1-1 says RETIRE must land before Layer 1 — no AC enforces this. Phase 5 agents in parallel worktrees could commit in any order; if Layer 1 lands before RETIRE, `v6.9.0-webhook-proto-coverage.sh` fails the build mid-sequence and Autopilot's on-error:skip behavior strands the release in partial state.
findings.proposed_fix: Add meta-AC-ORDERING-1: 'git log --reverse --pretty=format:%H v6.9.2..HEAD -- <touched-files> SHALL place RETIRE commits before Layer 1 sed commits, Layer 1 sed commits before fixtures.sh commit, fixtures.sh before Layer 4 scenario.' Verify via commit-ordering test.
findings.phase_7_failure_mode: Parallel execution or out-of-order rebase produces a broken intermediate state; CI fails; rollback is messy because exit-77 was added to the wrong scenarios relative to sed-affected ones.

findings.summary: Multiple ACs use 'non-trivial bash/jq logic additions' (AC-T1-2-1), 'only whitespace/lint changes' (AC-T1-16-1) — these thresholds are subjective. 'At least one of: jq -n, jq -e, jq -r, bash =~' is a better pattern, used correctly in some ACs. 'Non-trivial' and 'whitespace/lint' are aspirational wordings that a Phase 8 reviewer cannot mechanically verify.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace 'non-trivial' with exact predicate (e.g., '≥ 15 lines added, contains at least one of: jq -n, jq -e/-r, bash =~, function declaration, process substitution < <(').

findings.summary: Scope jumped from Judge recommendation (top-5 REWRITE) to User Gate 1 Override (14 REWRITE). Spec acknowledges the override. Track 3 scope went from roadmap's 8 to judge's 11 (NOT a user override — spec presents this as 'judge synthesis'). The Gate 1 decision document sits at `.forge/phase-3-brainstorm/gate-decision.json` (referenced but not read during review for isolation). The spec TEXT does not cite the specific user override quote/phrase. Phase 5 agents will re-derive scope from the spec alone; a reviewer who disagrees cannot trace back to authority.
findings.proposed_fix: Quote the User Gate 1 decision verbatim in requirements.md §1 (even 1-2 lines). Cite Phase 3 Decision #1 (11 agents) with line reference.

top_5_one_liners: CRITICAL DA-1: REQ-T1-1 says 'exactly 5 RETIRE' but lists 4 and freezes at 4 — a Phase 5 agent will add a phantom 5th scenario.
CRITICAL DA-2: Phase 2 ground-truth classification (KEEP=14/REWRITE=16/EXTEND=8/RETIRE=3) contradicts spec's (13/14/8/4) with 2 scenarios silently orphaned.
CRITICAL DA-3: Anti-pattern gate enumeration scope is INCONSISTENT across REQ-T1-5, AC-T1-5-1, REQ-T1-7, design §4.2 — three different scopes for the same gate.
CRITICAL DA-4: AC-T2-8-1 'declares HIGH confidence' is untestable — research artifact schema undefined, Phase 5 agent can stamp HIGH on an empty file.
CRITICAL DA-5: REQ-T2-3 '42 dispatch sites' is not enumerated by line number in-spec; 'drift-tolerant lower bound' is not a freeze.
recommendations_to_phase_4_planner: Re-write REQ-T1-1 to match AC-T1-1-1 (exactly 4). Move reconciliation note to Revision Notes.
Append a ledger: all 41 v6.9.0-prefixed scenarios with explicit KEEP/EXTEND/REWRITE/RETIRE classification; resolve orphan status of pipeline-history-pii-scope and pipeline-paused-webhook.
Define the 'v6.10.0-touched scenario list' as a single normative appendix; REQ-T1-7, AC-T1-5-1, design §4.2 must all reference it by name. Generate it from git diff, not hand-maintain.
Define research-artifact schema (frontmatter with confidence, ≥2 citations per gap, mandatory section headings). Rewrite AC-T2-8-1 to grep that schema.
Enumerate all 42 dispatch sites as (file, line-number) tuples in an appendix. Change AC-T2-2-1 from aggregate count to per-site coverage.
Specify Phase 8 Commander gate-execution protocol: exact command, exact capture field in report.md, cross-check with harness log.
Reconcile harness total: 186 vs 187 vs 204 — pick one, adjust NFR-10 effort budget accordingly. Do NOT ship 19 net-new scenarios on a 30-hour Track 1 budget.
Make T2-ADV-3 disclosure unconditional, not research-outcome-dependent.
Decide parse_pause_timeout REWRITE strategy explicitly (REQ-T1-19) and budget the hours.
Add meta-AC for commit ordering via git log --reverse.
